methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $157M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
BRINC Drones, founded by Blake Resnick, is a Series C-stage defense and security company that has raised $157M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 same-sector peers at comparable and adjacent stages, comprising 8 private companies and 2 public listings (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Key private comparables include Saronic, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Flock Safety.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($157M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical Series C-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.7x, implies a value of $267M. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company comp set yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M (low) to $9.2B (high, Saronic at Series D). The wide peer range reflects meaningful stage and business-model dispersion within defense/security infrastructure.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $227M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $821M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables drive the upper range. Key uncertainties include BRINC's relative technology maturity and market traction versus named peers, both of which would shift the result meaningfully within the stated range. The 7x spread between low and high reflects genuine ambiguity in how the market values this stage and sector.
